title: Block Chance System
problem: blockChance is a stat on items but has zero effect in combat. Shields and high-dex builds have no mechanical identity. Block gives offhand/shield items a defensive purpose distinct from raw defense.
analysis: blockChance stat exists in STAT_DESC with description 'Chance to fully block an incoming attack' but is never read in combat.ts. offhand equipment slot exists. The block mechanic gives the offhand slot a clear mechanical identity — a shield build trades damage output for survival via blocks. This pairs with hit/miss: miss is attacker-side failure, block is defender-side success.
implementation: 1. combat.ts: after monster attack hits (post-miss check), roll blockChance% — on block: set incomingDamage = 0, result includes isBlocked: true. 2. blockChance source: playerStats.blockChance from equipped items. 3. Passive offhand bonus: if player has offhand equipped and it has no blockChance stat, grant 10% base block (holding anything in off-hand is slightly defensive). 4. Block cap enforced at 75%. 5. roundToText: show 'Blocked!' when isBlocked. 6. Block only vs physical attacks — elemental damage is unblockable (goes around the shield). 7. PlayerCombatStats: add blockChance field. 8. buildPlayerStats: read blockChance from equipped offhand primarily, also from any item with blockChance affix.

decisions.decision: Block resolves after the miss check — you can only block attacks that land
decisions.rationale: Keeps the two systems independent and readable. Miss = attacker failed. Block = defender succeeded. Sequential resolution avoids double-negation confusion in log.

decisions.decision: Passive 10% base block when any offhand is equipped, even without blockChance stat
decisions.rationale: Makes the offhand slot feel defensively meaningful even for non-block builds. Holding anything in your off-hand is slightly protective.
alternativesConsidered: Block only from explicit blockChance stat
Offhand grants 0% base, all block from affix

decisions.decision: Block cap at 75% — elemental damage unblockable
decisions.rationale: 75% physical block cap matches D2. Elemental bypass distinguishes enchanted monster threat profile and ensures resist gear remains relevant even for shield builds.
alternativesConsidered: Block applies to elemental at 50% effectiveness

successCriteria: Player with blockChance stat occasionally shows 'Blocked!' in combat log. Block never exceeds 75% regardless of item stacking. Elemental damage from enchanted monsters is never blocked. Equipping any offhand grants base 10% block. Block correctly reduces damage to 0.
knownLimitations: Monster blocking not implemented — only player blocks
No visual shield indicator beyond 'Blocked!' in combat log
Block% not shown as active metric in CombatScreen
recommendations: EquipmentPanel should highlight offhand slot with a shield icon when blockChance > 0. Add block% to CombatScreen stats display alongside HP.

publicInterface.name: isBlocked
publicInterface.description: True when player's block roll succeeds — incoming physical damage set to 0

publicInterface.name: blockChance
publicInterface.description: 0-75 percentage chance to block physical attacks; sourced from offhand item + affixes

codeSnippets.label: Block resolution
codeSnippets.snippet:
const effectiveBlock = Math.min(75, playerStats.blockChance ?? 0)
const isBlocked = !isElemental && rng() * 100 < effectiveBlock

codeSnippets.label: Passive offhand block

userGuide: Equipping an item in your off-hand slot gives you a base 10% chance to fully block incoming physical attacks. Items with the Block Chance stat increase this further, up to a maximum of 75%. When you block, the combat log shows 'Blocked!' and you take zero damage. Note: elemental damage (fire, cold, lightning) cannot be blocked — only physical hits.

codexEntry.title: Blocking
codexEntry.summary: Equipping an off-hand item gives you a base 10% chance to fully block physical attacks. Items with Block Chance raise it further.
mechanics: Block only triggers on physical attacks — elemental damage bypasses shields entirely
Block resolves AFTER the hit check — you can only block attacks that land
Base block: 10% when any off-hand is equipped (even without a blockChance stat)
Block cap: 75% regardless of how much blockChance you stack
A successful block sets incoming physical damage to 0
blockChance stat on items stacks additively with the base 10%
Formula: effective block = min(75, 10 (if offhand equipped) + sum of blockChance from all items)
tips: Any off-hand item is worth equipping for the free 10% block — even a weak one.
Block does nothing against Fire/Cold/Lightning Enchanted monsters' elemental portion — stack resists too.
The off-hand slot trade-off: dual-wield (damage) vs. shield (survival).
